title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Help Version Safety evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Help Version Safety assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 8.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. Competency probe: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Help Version Safety has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Help Version Safety precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 8.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. Competency probe: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A command from a current web page behaves identically on every installed Git version.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the debug Help Version Safety case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the debug Help Version Safety case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the debug Help Version Safety case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the debug Help Version Safety case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-help-version-safety-zrqqd7" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Help Version Safety into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-help-version-safety-zrqqd7
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Help Version Safety structural check is only one gate. a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Help Version Safety: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 6.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. Competency probe: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
steps.why: Retrieving Help Version Safety through the concrete a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
